name: Duck with Black Bean Sauce and Tamarind Jus
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the stuffing ingredients in the cavity of each duck.
2. Trim excess fat and tie the legs to the tail end.
3. Season with salt and pepper; roast at 325°F for 2 hours.
4. Pour off any grease when ducks are golden brown.
5. Remove from oven and cool to room temperature.
6. Halve the ducks along the backbone and debone; discard stuffing.
7. Simmer bones in water, onion, carrot, salt, and pepper for about 2 hours.
8. Reserve duck stock.
9. For black bean sauce:
a. Blanch beans until soft, then chop coarsely.
b. Puree skinned apple.
c. Sweat shallots in Port wine until translucent.
d. Combine with beans, pureed apple, and remaining ingredients; simmer 15 minutes.
e. Stir in butter; set aside.
10. For tamarind jus:
a. Soak tamarind in water until soft.
b. Strain pulp, discarding seeds.
c. Puree pulp and combine with duck stock; heat through.
d. Stir in butter.
11. Place skin-side down in roasting pan; roast at 500°F for 10 minutes (until crispy).
12. Serve with black bean sauce drizzled over the ducks and tamarind jus poured over dishes, accompanied by oriental vegetables.
